schema_source: grande_finale.tex \label{prob:saturated-bc} ("saturated primitive split-pencil line-ray target"); the first data/derivation packet on this promoted problem -- the exact margin identity, the budget-fit of the paid fixed-deficiency Conjecture-F strata, an exhaustive toy census of the dedup identity, and the reduction of the open core to prob:capg-split-pencil-B / prob:capfp-R1
deliverable_name: Saturated line-rays: the rank-one budget fit and the growing-dimension gap

cross_refs.prob:saturated-bc: grande_finale.tex L1768 -- the promoted target this packet is the first data/derivation packet on
cross_refs.thm:saturation: L1509 -- exact identity Cen(U;m) = sum_c C(s_c(U),m)
cross_refs.cor:raw-bc-fails: L1541 -- a raw support census can overcount a single MCA ray by C(n-d,m)
cross_refs.prop:line-ray-saturation: L1565 -- the two exact dedup losses this packet measures
cross_refs.thm:bc-proper: L1463 -- unconditional split-pencil dimension bound min(C(n,omega), |F|^{r1+r2})
cross_refs.prop:base-field-floor: L1261 -- interior base-field split-pencil floor B_B(a+)
cross_refs.raw:lem:capf-dim1: cap25_cap_v13_raw.tex L6696 -- P2 deficiency-1 bound floor(|H|/j)
cross_refs.raw:rem:capf-conjf-open: L6758 -- names the growing-dimensional incidence as unsettled, filed under prob:band (NOTE: laneM_analysis.md's Sec 6 cites this remark at 'raw L8225'; L8225 is actually inside rem:capfp-band-obstruction, a different remark -- see found_vs_claimed_corrections)
cross_refs.raw:prop:capfr1-detrep: L7992 -- determinantal representation of the adversary
cross_refs.raw:rem:capfp-gamma-measured: L8228 -- measured Gamma_r = 1+o(1) in the dense bulk
cross_refs.raw:prop:capfp-kernel: L8173 -- moment-kernel dictionary, the route-1 candidate closing Q and BC together
cross_refs.raw:lem:capfp-functionals: L8246 -- the S_r(f,T) criterion this packet's verifier uses independently
cross_refs.raw:prob:capfp-R1: L8278 -- slope-form of the missing lemma
cross_refs.raw:prob:capg-split-pencil-B: L9842 -- support-form of the missing lemma (task brief cites ~L9841; off-by-one, same remark)
cross_refs.raw:prob:band: L4624 -- the named open umbrella problem
cross_refs.#369: Latif Kasuli, OPEN -- KB-MCA L4 base-field floor ladder fixture; cited for the L4 cross-check only, not duplicated
cross_refs.#378: Holm Buar, OPEN -- Lean BC interior census floor (lower side only); sibling result, not duplicated

margins_table.note: flatness rung, no deployed B*; see l4_consistency block

margin_identity: margin = log2(B*) - log2(C(n,a+) * p^-w); B* = floor(p^spow / 2^lam); confirmed to reproduce grande_finale.tex's own printed table (the 'adjacent agreement / spare margin' table) to all 4 printed decimals

P1_extremal_pin.statement: prop:rank-one-distinct-slope-floor's constructed non-B-rational pole line realizes >= N - C(N,2)(K-1)/(q-|B|) distinct MCA-bad slopes, N = ceil(C(n,a+)|B|^-w) = B_B(a+); at the frontier N~2^35.7 << (q-|B|)/(K-1) ~ 2^166, so the count is >= B_B(a+)*(1-o(1)); combined with the trivial image<=domain bound N_slopes<=N, the constructed extremal class is two-sidedly pinned at B_B(a+) = B* * 2^-Delta, Delta in {22.1969, 22.0109, 3.2589, 3.0730}
P1_extremal_pin.status: PROVED-LOCAL(corollary of cited unconditional propositions) -- fixes the TARGET CONSTANT for a general upper bound; is NOT itself a general upper bound

P2_fixed_deficiency_ledger.note: against a ~22-bit margin, the two proved bounds are 10^5x-10^7x too weak in the exponent; this IS the open core

toy_census.protocol: exhaustive line-ray census over F_{p^2} >= B=F_p >= D, all q slopes, 3 line families (random_base, pole [adversarial/extension-valued], random_ext) x 5 (p,K,m) configs x 3 seeds = 45 instances
toy_census.source_script: laneM_compute.py PART B (packaging input, output.txt); independently re-implemented in verify_saturated_bc_budget_fit.py gate iii via the S_r(f,T) functional criterion of lem:capfp-functionals (a different algorithm from laneM_compute.py's Newton-divided-difference approach)

headline_adversarial_witness.note: 64 of 71 bad slopes are extension-valued -- the prop:rank-one-distinct-slope-floor signature in miniature; adversarial pole lines maximize N_slopes and skew it extension-valued

generic_witness.note: generic F-valued lines are clean: loss1=loss2=0, each bad slope has exactly one saturated ray at exactly agreement m

toy_census.census_overcount_factor_observed: ~2-9x already at n=13 (e.g. random_base p=13: Cen=119 vs nbad=13; pole p=13: Cen=135 vs nbad=71)
toy_census.pre_asymptotic_caveat: at these toy sizes q=p^2 is small, so the collision term C(N,2)(K-1)/(q-b) is NOT negligible and the pole line's nbad (71) can exceed the base-field floor M_B (11) -- the complement of the frontier pin (Sec 2.2 of laneM_analysis.md). At the deployed rows q=p^6 >> N*K, so the pin holds and N_slopes ~ M_B(a+). The toy demonstrates the MECHANISM, not the frontier constant.

support_form.label: prob:capg-split-pencil-B
support_form.statement: #{(A,B): deg A<=omega-d1, deg B<=omega-d2, A*W1+B*W2 | Lambda_D, deg(A*W1+B*W2)=omega} <= e^{o(n)} * max(1, M_B(d1), C(n,omega)*q^{1-w}), counting monic normalizations, with M_B(d1) = C(K-1+d1,m) * C(n,K-1+d1) * p^{-(d1-1)} the subfield floor of prop:capg-census-floor, achieved there

slope_form.label: prob:capfp-R1
slope_form.location: cap25_cap_v13_raw.tex L8278; corrected base-field model at L9860-9861 (inside prob:capg-split-pencil-B's own remark)
slope_form.statement: N_slopes <= #R1(u,v;m) <= e^{o(n)} * max(1, C(n,m)*p^{-w}, C(n,m)*q^{-(w-1)}), the middle term achieved by the non-B-rational pole lines of prop:capg-census-floor(c)

finite_deployed_instance.name: (BC-fin)
finite_deployed_instance.statement: R_BC <= 2^Delta with Delta = 22.1969 (KB-MCA) / 3.2589 (M31-MCA), i.e. N_slopes <= 2^Delta * B_B(a+) <= B* after the first-match ledger subtracts the already-paid cells

moment_kernel_route.text: if Gamma_r = e^{o(n)} persists to r ~ 10^5 (prob:capfp-gamma, raw L8135), the split-pencil census inherits the |B|^{-w} normalization by the moment method -- this is the one object that would close Q and BC on the same {0,+-1}-configuration count Gamma_r

cross_checks.source: this packet's own from-scratch recompute (verify_saturated_bc_budget_fit.py gate iv)

kb_mca_conjq_rung_audit_v1.json (already integrated on main), rung j=4, field log2_quotient_avg_a_j -- a DIFFERENT computational route (quotient-average pigeonhole of the conj:Q rung audit, not the direct base-field-floor formula used here)

PR #369 (Latif Kasuli, open), verify_bc_l4_base_floor_ladder.py diff, base_field_floor_log2_approx at d1=4217 (prop:capg-census-floor's direct route) -- confirmed via `gh pr diff 369` during packaging, not fetched live by the verifier

l4_consistency.agreement: all three agree to six decimal places (23.139009...); a three-route cross-validation of one exact rational, not a proof about prob:saturated-bc

found_vs_claimed_corrections.item: dim W (Conjecture-F incidence dimension) at the current moved adjacent pair
found_vs_claimed_corrections.claimed_in: laneM_analysis.md Sec 2.3 and Sec 3, quoting raw prob:capfp-balanced (cap25_cap_v13_raw.tex L8386): 'dimension ... (913642 at the KoalaBear adjacent pair, 913686 at Mersenne-31)'
found_vs_claimed_corrections.found: recomputing dim W = omega - w + 1 at the CURRENT active moved-pair a_plus values (1116048 KB-MCA / 1116024 M31-MCA, per rem:packet-convention) gives 913634 (KB) / 913682 (M31), matching output.txt's own d=omega-w (913633/913681) plus one. No integer m within +/-3 of either current a_0/a_plus reproduces 913642/913686 under either K choice; raw prob:capfp-balanced's printed figure appears to be a leftover from a historical (pre-move) adjacent-pair numbering elsewhere in the same v13 raw manuscript -- exactly the phenomenon rem:packet-convention documents for other tables in the same packet family. This packet and its note use 913634/913682 throughout, not 913642/913686.
found_vs_claimed_corrections.action: not silently fixed in laneM_analysis.md (a Lane M working file, not part of this PR); flagged here and in the companion note instead

found_vs_claimed_corrections.claimed_in: laneM_analysis.md Sec 6: 'the manuscript itself files under prob:band (rem:capf-conjf-open, raw L8225)'
found_vs_claimed_corrections.found: rem:capf-conjf-open's actual \label line is cap25_cap_v13_raw.tex L6758 (confirmed by direct grep and by reading its content, which matches the Sec 2/Sec 3 citation of the same label elsewhere in laneM_analysis.md). Raw L8225 is mid-paragraph inside rem:capfp-band-obstruction (a different remark, starting at L8220), not rem:capf-conjf-open.
found_vs_claimed_corrections.action: this packet cites rem:capf-conjf-open at L6758 only

status: AUDIT / EXACT_ARITHMETIC(margins, PART A) / PROVED-LOCAL(P1, P2 as corollaries of cited unconditional lemmas) / MEASURED(toy census, PART B, 45 instances) / OPEN(the growing-dimension core = prob:capg-split-pencil-B / prob:capfp-R1 / prob:band). This packet does not prove prob:saturated-bc, does not certify U(a0+1)<=B* for the growing-deficiency interior cell, and does not supersede PR #369's L4 fixture or PR #378's Lean BC lower-side floors.
